metadata.seed: Yeah. Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I mean, I feel lucky as a woman in the 21st century to have a man who cares about her and takes her out and makes her feel appreciative. I've never had that before. And I go home happy. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

patient.query: Yeah. Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I mean, I feel lucky as a woman in the 21st century to have a man who cares about her and takes her out and makes her feel appreciative. I've never had that before. And I go home happy. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

automatic_thoughts: I can't figure out why I'm so bothered
there are criticisms about my relationship
it's weird for a 58 year old man to be with me
age is an issue for others but not for me
behaviors: going home excited to see him

neutral: Fear of reaction regarding family planning choices (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly frustrating to have these doubts and criticisms surface when you know, deep down, that you have something truly special with Stu. It's understandable that you're struggling to reconcile that with what others are saying. Perhaps we could explore what makes those external comments feel so impactful, and consider if there might be other ways to view the situation.

patient.query: Yeah, it really is frustrating. I just can't help but let those comments get under my skin sometimes. Like, I know Stu and I have something real, but when people are so vocal about their doubts, it just makes me second-guess everything. It’s hard to drown out that noise. I wish I could just feel confident about us without all this outside chatter.

automatic_thoughts: I can't help but let those comments get under my skin
I second-guess everything
I wish I could just feel confident about us

entailment: Tends to be sensitive to criticism (finding)
neutral: Tends to be critical of others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly frustrating to have your relationship questioned by others, especially when you know you have something special with Stu. It makes sense that you'd feel like second-guessing things when you're constantly hearing those doubts. It takes a lot of strength to navigate that kind of pressure.

patient.query: Thanks. I guess it does take a bit of strength, but sometimes I just feel... tired, you know? Like, I want to believe in us, but every negative comment feels like a little crack in my confidence. It's like I'm always on edge, waiting for someone to point out what’s wrong instead of celebrating what’s right.

automatic_thoughts: every negative comment cracks my confidence
I want to believe in us

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot right now, and it's understandable to feel tired when you're constantly bracing yourself for criticism. What makes you feel that a negative comment is like a crack in your confidence?

patient.query: I suppose it just hits a nerve. I’ve always been the kind of person who worries about what others think, and when they doubt my choices, it feels like I’m not just defending my relationship but also myself. It’s hard to shake that feeling of inadequacy, especially when I already struggle with trusting my own instincts. Sometimes, I just want to scream that I’m trying my best!

automatic_thoughts: I need to defend my relationship
I am inadequate
I can't trust my instincts
I am trying my best

entailment: Tends to be sensitive to criticism (finding)
neutral: Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)
Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like these doubts from others bring up a lot for you, and it's understandable that you'd feel the need to defend yourself. What makes you feel like you need to defend yourself so strongly when others question your choices?

patient.query: Yeah, it really is a lot. I guess it’s just that I’ve been told before that I’m too sensitive or that I overthink things. So when people question my choices, it feels like they’re confirming that I don’t know what I'm doing. It triggers this feeling that I might be failing, and I just want to prove them wrong. It’s exhausting, honestly.

automatic_thoughts: I'm too sensitive
I overthink things
they're confirming I don't know what I'm doing
I might be failing
I want to prove them wrong

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)
Tends to be sensitive to criticism (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like those past experiences have really left a mark, and it's understandable that you're feeling exhausted. What do you think it would be like to consider that maybe those past comments weren't necessarily a reflection of your abilities? Perhaps we could explore that a little further.

patient.query: I guess that could help, but it’s hard for me to separate my feelings from what people say, you know? It just feels like I’m constantly measuring myself against their words. Sometimes I wish I could just brush it off, but it always seems to stick with me. Digging deeper feels daunting, like opening old wounds again. I really want to let go of that weight, but I’m not sure how to start.

automatic_thoughts: I can't separate my feelings
I wish I could brush it off
it always seems to stick with me
digging deeper feels daunting
I want to let go of that weight

llm_response.response:
REASONING:
The patient is expressing difficulty managing the impact of external feedback on their emotional state, indicating a pattern of self-evaluation based on others' opinions. They express a desire to detach from these feelings but feel overwhelmed by the process. The clinical context highlights difficulty understanding emotions and emotional adjustment. Given the patient's desire to let go of the "weight" and the daunting feeling of "digging deeper," a gentle exploration of the underlying assumptions driving this pattern would be beneficial. Socratic questioning seems appropriate here to encourage self-awareness and independent problem-solving.

FINAL RESPONSE:
It sounds like you're carrying a heavy burden, feeling very affected by what others say and struggling to shake it off. What do you think it is about those words that makes them linger and feel so impactful? Perhaps exploring that a little further could offer some clarity.

patient.query: It’s just... I think a lot of it comes from my past. There were times when I felt like I was never enough, and it’s like those old feelings resurface whenever someone doubts me now. It’s almost like I can’t help but replay those moments in my head. I want to shake them off, but it feels like they’ve just stuck around, you know?

automatic_thoughts: I was never enough
old feelings resurface
I can't help but replay those moments

neutral: Ability to resist obsessional thoughts (observable entity)

llm_response.response: It sounds like those past experiences have had a lasting impact, and it's understandable that those feelings would resurface when you feel doubted. Sometimes, when we've felt that way before, our minds can automatically jump to those old conclusions. Perhaps we could explore whether there might be other ways to understand what someone's doubt might mean in this situation?

patient.query: It’s just... I think a lot of it comes from my past. There were times when I felt like I was never enough, and it’s like those old feelings resurface whenever someone doubts me now. I can’t help but replay those moments in my head. I want to shake them off, but it feels like they’ve just stuck around, you know?

automatic_thoughts: I was never enough
those old feelings resurface
I can't shake them off
behaviors: replay those moments in my head

llm_response.response: It sounds like those past experiences have left a lasting impact, and it's understandable that those feelings would resurface when you feel doubted. Can you tell me a little more about what those moments from the past were like, and what made you feel like you weren't enough?

patient.query: Yeah, I guess it all goes back to family. There were always these high expectations, and no matter how hard I tried, it felt like I was never living up to them. I remember times when my accomplishments just didn’t get the praise I wanted, or even worse, were overshadowed by someone else’s. So I ended up feeling like love and approval were something I had to earn, not something I just got for being me. It’s like I’m always chasing that validation, even

automatic_thoughts: I'm never living up to expectations
I have to earn love and approval
I'm always chasing validation

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like your family experiences have significantly shaped how you view yourself and your relationships. Can you tell me more about what it was like when you felt you weren't meeting those expectations? What thoughts or feelings came up for you in those moments?

patient.query: It was just so frustrating, honestly. I remember feeling this heavy weight on my shoulders, like I was constantly under a spotlight, waiting to be judged. I would think, “Why can’t I just be enough?” It made me feel so small and ashamed, like I was failing not just them, but myself too. There was always this nagging fear that if I didn’t succeed, I wouldn’t be loved. It’s like those moments stuck with me, shaping how I see everything now

automatic_thoughts: Why can’t I just be enough?
if I didn’t succeed, I wouldn’t be loved

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
neutral: Enduring personality change after catastrophic experience (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’ve been carrying a tremendous amount of pressure and self-doubt. It's understandable that you'd feel frustrated and ashamed when you're constantly questioning whether you're "enough." Perhaps, if a friend were experiencing this, what would you tell them about their worth?
